2604.07180.title: Energy-based Tissue Manifolds for Longitudinal Multiparametric MRI Analysis
2604.07180.paper_summary: We propose a geometric framework for longitudinal multi-parametric MRI analysis based on patient-specific energy modelling in sequence space. Rather than operating on images with spatial networks, each voxel is represented by its multi-sequence intensity vector ($T1$, $T1c$, $T2$, FLAIR, ADC), and a compact implicit neural representation is trained via denoising score matching to learn an energy function $E_θ(\mathbf{u})$ over $\mathbb{R}^d$ from a single baseline scan. The learned energy landscape provides a differential-geometric description of tissue regimes without segmentation labels. Local minima define tissue basins, gradient magnitude reflects proximity to regime boundaries, and Laplacian curvature characterises local constraint structure. Importantly, this baseline energy manifold is treated as a fixed geometric reference: it encodes the set of contrast combinations observed at diagnosis and is not retrained at follow-up. Longitudinal assessment is therefore formulated as evaluation of subsequent scans relative to this baseline geometry. Rather than comparing anatomical segmentations, we analyse how the distribution of MRI sequence vectors evolves under the baseline energy function. In a paediatric case with later recurrence, follow-up scans show progressive deviation in energy and directional displacement in sequence space toward the baseline tumour-associated regime before clear radiological reappearance. In a case with stable disease, voxel distributions remain confined to established low-energy basins without systematic drift. The presented cases serve as proof-of-concept that patient-specific energy manifolds can function as geometric reference systems for longitudinal mpMRI analysis without explicit segmentation or supervised classification, providing a foundation for further investigation of manifold-based tissue-at-risk tracking in neuro-oncology.
2604.07180.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個基於患者特定能量建模的幾何框架，用於縱向多參數MRI分析，該框架在序列空間中運作。與其在具有空間網絡的圖像上進行操作，每個體素由其多序列強度向量 ($T1$, $T1c$, $T2$, FLAIR, ADC) 表示，並通過去噪聲分數匹配訓練一個緊湊的隱式神經表示，以從單一基線掃描中學習能量函數 $E_θ(\mathbf{u})$ 在 $\mathbb{R}^d$ 上。學習到的能量景觀提供了一種無需分割標籤的組織區域的微分幾何描述。局部極小值定義了組織盆地，梯度大小反映了與區域邊界的接近程度，而拉普拉斯曲率則表徵了局部約束結構。重要的是，這個基線能量流形被視為固定的幾何參考：它編碼了在診斷時觀察到的對比組合集，並且在隨訪時不會重新訓練。因此，縱向評估被表述為相對於這個基線幾何的後續掃描的評估。與其比較解剖分割，我們分析MRI序列向量在基線能量函數下的分佈如何演變。在一個後來復發的兒科案例中，隨訪掃描顯示能量的逐步偏離和在序列空間中朝向基線腫瘤相關區域的方向位移，直至明顯的放射學重現。在一個穩定病情的案例中，體素分佈保持在已建立的低能量盆地內，沒有系統性漂移。所呈現的案例作為概念驗證，證明患者特定的能量流形可以作為縱向mpMRI分析的幾何參考系統，而無需明確的分割或監督分類，為在神經腫瘤學中進一步研究基於流形的風險組織追蹤提供了基礎。
2604.07180.author: Kartikay Tehlan et.al.
2604.07180.authors: Kartikay Tehlan, Lukas Förner, Nico Schmutzenhofer, Michael Frühwald, Matthias Wagner, Nassir Navab, Thomas Wendler
